text.title: Conversational Pattern Failure Modes
text.summary: Five antipatterns that recur in conversational multi-agent runs, paired with detector + fix.

antipattern.description: Missing or weak termination_phrase — the loop only ever exits via max_turns.
antipattern.reason: Detect: trace shows all runs end with `turn == max_turns` and no termination phrase fired. Fix: declare a concrete phrase ("TASK COMPLETE", "FINAL ANSWER:"); instruct one designated agent to emit it when the conversation goal is met.

antipattern.description: max_turns set absurdly high (> 30) "just to be safe" — runs that should have early-exited burn tokens until the cap.
antipattern.reason: Detect: validator flags `max_turns > 30`. Fix: cap at 30; if you legitimately need more turns, you have a `sequential` pipeline mis-labelled as conversational.

antipattern.description: Full conversation history passed to every turn prompt — token cost grows quadratically.
antipattern.reason: Detect: per-turn input tokens grow roughly linearly with turn index. Fix: pass only the last `window_size` (default 3) entries; previous content is lost — that is the intended trade.

antipattern.description: No per-turn budget audit — the run hits max_turns at any cost; budget is "monitored" only post-hoc.
antipattern.reason: Detect: post-run total_tokens far exceeds spec.budget. Fix: maintain `used_tokens` counter; abort the loop and emit `aborted_early=true` when `used_tokens ≥ 0.9 × budget`.

antipattern.description: Two agents with identical system_prompts — conversation degenerates to monologue or empty agreement.
antipattern.reason: Detect: turns from different agents have >0.9 pairwise cosine similarity. Fix: assign clearly opposing or complementary roles (proposer/critic, designer/security-reviewer), put the opposition into the system prompt itself.
